channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Third-party device compliance partners support in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use a third-party device compliance partner as a source of compliance data for devices you manage with Intune.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS App Protection Policy Settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes the iOS/iPadOS app protection policy (APP) settings for iOS/iPadOS devices.

item.title: Vulnerability Remediation Agent Overview and Set Up - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Vulnerability Remediation Agent in Microsoft Intune, its prerequisites, how it works, and how to set it up. The agent uses AI to identify and prioritize vulnerability remediation based on Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management data.

item.title: In development - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes Microsoft Intune features that are in development.

item.title: Understand Microsoft Intune Management Extension - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand Microsoft Intune management extension for Windows.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Network endpoints for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review endpoints for Intune. This page lists IP addresses and port settings needed for proxy settings in your Intune deployments.

item.title: Deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Follow the steps to deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Using Remote Help on Windows to Assist Authenticated Users - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Remote Help app to provide remote assistance to authenticated users who also run the Remote Help app, and to troubleshoot for frontline workers (FLW).

item.title: Add an Unmanaged macOS PKG App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add an unmanaged macOS PKG app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Prerequisites the Microsoft Tunnel VPN for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the prerequisites for installation and use of the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. These include Linux servers, network, and firewall configurations.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Enterprise Application Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Enterprise App Management and the Enterprise App Catalog in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Plan for Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the requirements and capabilities of Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Supported Microsoft Intune Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides lists of support partner and Microsoft apps that are commonly used with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up enrollment time grouping - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview and setup of the enrollment time grouping feature in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Learn about Intune security baselines for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy security baselines that have preset and recommended configurations to the Windows devices you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Default configuration of Intune's Windows security baselines - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the default setting configuration of the various Microsoft Intune security baselines for Windows.

item.title: Add an Enterprise App Catalog App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add an Enterprise App Catalog app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Wi-Fi Settings for Apple Devices in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or create a Wi-Fi configuration profile on iOS/iPadOS and macOS devices using Wi-Fi configuration settings in Microsoft Intune. Configure the connection details, authentication methods, SSIDs, security types, and proxy settings.

item.title: Use OEMConfig on Android Enterprise devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage and use devices running Android Enterprise with OEMConfig. See all the steps, including an overview, see the prerequisites, create the configuration profile in Intune, and see a list of supported OEMConfig apps.

item.title: List of settings for the Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise security baseline in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View a list of the settings in the Microsoft Intune security baseline for Microsoft Office apps. This list includes the default values for settings as found in the default configuration of the baseline.

item.title: Mobile Threat Defense with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) with your Mobile Threat Defense partner to protect access to company resources based on device risk.

item.title: Android configuration list for Intune settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune settings catalog to add, configure, or restrict features on Android devices. This article lists and describes the settings you can configure.

item.title: Add and assign MTD apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune to add Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) apps, Microsoft Authenticator app, and iOS configuration policy in Microsoft Intune

item.title: Set up Trustd Mobile Threat Defense integration with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: How to set up Trustd Mobile Threat Defense with Microsoft Intune to control mobile device access to your corporate resources.

item.title: Trustd Mobile Threat Defense with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Trustd Mobile Threat Defense with Microsoft Intune to control mobile device access to your corporate resources.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Government Service overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Intune government service offerings and features. This article is designed to serve as an overview of the Microsoft Intune offering for government community cloud (GCC) High and United States Department of Defense (DoD) environments.

item.title: Configure Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Intune and onboard devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Connect Microsoft Defender for Endpoint to Intune, onboard devices by platform, and configure compliance and Conditional Access policies that use device risk levels to control access to corporate resources.

item.title: Integrate Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Intune for Device Compliance - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Integrate Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Microsoft Intune as a Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) solution to enforce device compliance and prevent security breaches.

item.title: Deploy and configure Microsoft Defender for Endpoint on Android - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to deploy and configure Microsoft Defender for Endpoint on Android devices using Microsoft Intune, including app configuration policies, always-on VPN, and low-touch onboarding.

item.title: Use Intune to remediate vulnerabilities found by Microsoft Defender for Endpoint - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Integrate Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management with Intune to create security tasks, track remediation, and reduce vulnerability exposure across managed devices.

item.title: Configure Defender for Endpoint web protection on Android devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune policy to manage Microsoft Defender for Endpoint web protection settings on Android devices managed by Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Monitor Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to monitor device compliance and onboarding status for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Microsoft Intune in the admin center.

item.title: Configure Email settings for iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of all the email settings you can configure and add to iOS and iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune, including using Exchange servers, and getting attributes from Microsoft Entra ID. You can also enable SSL, authenticate users with certificates or username/password, and synchronize email on iOS/iPadOS devices using device configuration profiles in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Update Windows BIOS features using DFCI MDM policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Device Firmware Configuration Interface (DFCI) profile to manage UEFI settings in Microsoft Intune. To use DFCI profiles, create Microsoft Entra security groups, the Windows Autopilot deployment profile, and the Enrollment State Page profile.

item.title: Device firmware configuration interface settings for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of all the DFCI profile settings and their descriptions on Windows 10/11 client devices. Use these settings in a configuration profile to control UEFI firmware layer features using Microsoft Intune policy. You can manage the CPU, built-in hardware, and boot options on client devices using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Email settings for Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create a device configuration email profile that that uses Exchange servers, and retrieves attributes from Microsoft Entra ID. You can also enable SSL, and synchronize email and schedules on Windows 10/11 client devices using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Windows upgrade and S mode settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of all the settings, and what they do when upgrading a Windows edition on a device, or switch out of S mode on a device using a device configuration profile in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Android Enterprise email settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create device configuration email profiles that use Exchange servers, and retrieve attributes from Microsoft Entra ID. Enable SSL or SMIME, authenticate users with certificates or username/password, and synchronize email and schedules on Android Enterprise personally owned devices with a work profile using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Kiosk settings for Windows Holographic for Business in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure your Windows Holographic for Business devices as single-app and multi-app kiosks, customize the start menu, add apps, show the task bar, and configure a web browser in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Join your cloud-native endpoints to Microsoft Entra - Microsoft Intune
item.description: When moving to or using cloud-native endpoints, use Microsoft Entra joined endpoints. When your endpoints are joined to Microsoft Entra, you can use Windows Autopilot to provision or get devices ready for organization use. Learn more about the benefits to IT admins and end-users.

item.title: Get started with macOS endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune guide to set up and configure macOS devices from setup to creating policies and enrolling devices. You secure your corporate and organization-owned endpoints that are enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager.

item.title: Avoid policy conflicts - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to avoid policy conflicts when creating and deploying new policies.

item.title: Use Multi Admin Approval with the Microsoft Graph API - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to update your automation scripts and applications to work with Multi Admin Approval enforcement on app-authenticated API calls in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up Microsoft Entra ID - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to create and prepare your Microsoft Entra tenant for an education environment.

item.title: Configure applications with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure applications with Microsoft Intune in preparation for device deployment.

item.title: What are cloud-native Windows endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about cloud-native endpoints and what they are. See a list of benefits, and the effect on end users and IT administrators. Cloud-native endpoints help with remote workers and hybrid workers, and use Microsoft Intune to manage devices.

item.title: Control AI features on Android Enterprise devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Using Microsoft Intune, you can manage and restrict AI usage on Android devices enrolled in Intune. This guide provides lists the steps in Intune to block AI apps, websites, screen-driven experiences, on-device AI services, and OEM-specific AI features. You can manage Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Samsung Galaxy AI, claude.ai, ChatGPT, and more.

item.title: Reset and wipe Education devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the reset and wipe options for Windows devices using Intune for Education, including scenarios when to delete devices.

item.title: Update your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To support hybrid and remote workers, convert or migrate your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints. This planning guide focuses on deploying apps and updates with Intune, moving from Group Policy Objects, and using Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Get started with iOS/iPadOS frontline worker devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage frontline worker devices using iOS and iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune. Select the best enrollment option, configure the home screen, and more.

item.title: Set up device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure the Intune service and set up the environment for education.

item.title: Get started with Android frontline worker devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage frontline worker devices using Android devices in Microsoft Intune. Select the best enrollment option, configure the home screen, and more.

item.title: Manage devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of device management capabilities in Intune for Education, including remote actions, remote assistance, and inventory/reporting.

item.title: Configure and secure devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure policies with Microsoft Intune in preparation for device deployment.

item.title: Plan Education device grouping and targeting - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan how you'll group devices and users and target policies and applications.

item.title: Get started with Windows frontline worker devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage frontline worker devices using Windows devices in Microsoft Intune. Select the best enrollment option, configure the home screen, and more.

item.title: Use Multi Admin Approval in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure Multi Admin Approval to protect your tenant against the use of compromised administrative accounts in Intune.

item.title: Get Started With the Microsoft Intune App SDK - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Quickly enable your mobile app for mobile application management (MAM) with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage Microsoft 365 (Office) for iOS and Android With Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune app protection and configuration policies with Microsoft 365 (Office) for iOS and Android to ensure collaboration experiences are always accessed with safeguards in place.

item.title: Add Platform SSO policy to ADE Profile on macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add a settings catalog platform single sign-on (PSSO) policy to an Automated Device Enrollment (ADE) profile and configure it to run during Setup Assistant with modern authentication on macOS devices.

item.title: Security Copilot in Intune features overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Security Copilot in Intune is an AI platform. It can help you create policies, get information about existing policies, and show more details on specific settings, including their impacts on users and devices. You can also use Copilot to troubleshoot device issues and query devices.

item.title: Planning guide to move to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan, design, implement, adopt, and move to Microsoft Intune. Get guidance and advice to determine goals, use-case scenarios and requirements, and create rollout and communication plans, support, testing, and validation plans.

item.title: Device restriction settings for Android in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: On Android Enterprise or Android for Work devices owned by your organization, you can restrict settings on the device using Microsoft Intune. Allow copy and paste, notifications, app permissions, data sharing, password length, sign in failures, use fingerprint to unlock, reuse passwords, and enable bluetooth sharing of work contacts. Configure devices as a dedicated device kiosk to run one app, or multiple apps.

item.title: Assign device profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune admin center to assign device configuration profiles and policies to users and devices. Learn how to exclude groups from a profile assignment in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Single sign-on (SSO) for iOS/iPadOS and macOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of Microsoft Enterprise SSO plug-in in Microsoft Intune, Jamf Pro, and other MDM solution providers. The Enterprise SSO plug-in is available on iOS/iPadOS and macOS devices.

item.title: Migration guide to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deployment guide to set up, onboard, or move to Intune. These steps include moving from partner MDM providers, using co-management, moving from on-premises group policy, and moving from Office 365 device management.

item.title: Platform SSO scenarios for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to configure common Platform SSO scenarios for macOS devices. You can enable Kerberos SSO to on-premises Active Directory and cloud-based Microsoft Entra ID, use Touch ID biometric policy with Secure Enclave authentication, and enable SSO on non-Microsoft apps. You can also configure end user experience settings.

item.title: Set permissions to Managed Home Screen using Android Enterprise OEMConfig - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add the Samsung Knox Service Plugin, Zebra OEMConfig Powered by MX, Legacy Zebra OEMConfig, and Honeywell UEMConnect apps to Intune, and use the app schemas to configure permissions for the Managed Home Screen (MHS) app on Android Enterprise devices.

item.title: Assignment Filter Performance Tips for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Optimize Microsoft Intune performance with assignment filters. Learn to use virtual groups, reuse groups, and apply filters effectively. Improve policy deployment speed with incremental group changes, and use assignment filters to include and exclude.

item.title: Deploy OEMConfig profiles to Zebra devices using Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to create and deploy multiple OEMConfig device configuration profiles on Zebra devices running Android Enterprise. Use Zebra actions and steps to order your profiles.

item.title: Platforms and policy types supported by assignment filters - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn which apps, compliance policies, and device configuration profiles and their platforms support assignment filters in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Platform SSO for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to configure Platform SSO and deploy the configuration to your macOS devices. Platform SSO enables single sign-on (SSO) using Microsoft Entra ID with the Secure Enclave, smart card, or password authentication methods. You create a settings catalog policy to configure the settings. This article is a step-by-step guide to configure Platform SSO for macOS devices using Intune.

item.title: Step 5 – Enroll devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune.

item.title: Get an Apple MDM Push certificate for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an Apple MDM Push certificate to manage iOS/iPadOS devices with Intune.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll iOS and iPadOS devices using user and device enrollment, automated device enrollment (DEP), and Apple Configurator in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Add the Company Portal for macOS App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add the macOS Company Portal app.

item.title: Learn about using Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To enhance the security of your organization, set your users to run with standard permissions while Endpoint Privilege Management ensures those users can seamlessly run specified files with elevated rights.

item.title: Configure iOS/iPadOS Enterprise SSO app extension with MDMs - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Microsoft Enterprise single sign-on (SSO) plug-in. Add or create an iOS or iPadOS device configuration profile using the Microsoft Enterprise SSO plug-in app extension in Microsoft Intune, Jamf Pro, and other MDM solution providers.

item.title: App Protection Policies Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Microsoft Intune app protection policies help protect your company data and prevent data loss.

item.title: Use Remediations to Detect and Fix Support Issues - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about Remediations in Microsoft Intune, including what Remediations are and view any prerequisites and licensing requirements. Also, learn how to deploy built-in and custom remediation scripts, and learn how to monitor your scripts.

item.title: Windows device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Windows devices using Automatic enrollment, Windows Autopilot, group policy, and co-management enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Licensing Plans and Options - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune licensing options, plans, and the capabilities included with each Intune plan and Microsoft 365 license tier.

item.title: Enroll devices using a device enrollment manager account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device enrollment manager account to enroll devices in Intune.

item.title: Encrypt Windows devices with BitLocker using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune policy to manage BitLocker encryption on Windows devices, including silent encryption and Personal Data Encryption.

item.title: Create a policy using settings catalog in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to configure thousands of settings for Windows 10/11, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Android client devices, including Microsoft Office apps, Microsoft Edge, administrative templates (ADMX), and more. Add these settings in a device configuration profile to secure devices, and control different programs and features. Use Microsoft Copilot to get What If analysis, and learn more about each setting.

item.title: Use SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create and assign Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add and Assign Win32 Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add, assign, and manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Discovered Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand details about the detected apps that Intune found on a device.

item.title: Connect Intune account to managed Google Play account - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to connect your Intune account to your Managed Google Play account.

item.title: Device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of Microsoft Intune device compliance, including tenant-wide compliance policy settings and device compliance policies.

item.title: Apple device restriction settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, or create settings on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS devices to restrict features in Microsoft Intune. Create password requirements, control the locked screen, use built-in apps, add restricted or approved apps, handle bluetooth devices, connect to the cloud for backup and storage, enable kiosk mode, add domains, and control how users interact with the Safari web browser.

item.title: Manage Apple Volume-Purchased Apps - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to sync apps you bought in volume through Apple Business Manager with Microsoft Intune. Then manage and track these apps on iOS/iPadOS and macOS devices.

item.title: Collect Device Hardware Info With the Properties Catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the properties catalog in Microsoft Intune to collect device hardware info like BIOS version, TPM status, and disk details on managed Windows devices. Get deeper visibility into your device inventory and troubleshoot issues.

item.title: Create assignment filters in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create assignment filters in Microsoft Intune to target policies based on device properties like OS version or manufacturer. Learn to create, update, and delete filters for managed devices and apps.

item.title: Configure macOS Enterprise SSO app extension with MDMs - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Microsoft Enterprise single sign-on (SSO) app extension plug-in. Add or create an macOS device profile using the SSO app extension in Microsoft Intune, Jamf Pro, and other MDM solution providers.

item.title: Set up automated device enrollment (ADE) for iOS/iPadOS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll corporate-owned iOS/iPadOS devices into Microsoft Intune with Apple Automated Device Enrollment (ADE).

item.title: Add corporate identifiers to Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add corporate identifiers (enrollment method, IMEI, and serial numbers) to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set the mobile device management authority - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to set the mobile device management authority in Microsoft Intune and some key considerations.

item.title: Win32 App Management in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune. This article provides an overview of the Intune Win32 app delivery and management capabilities.
